LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JENNY YING HE AND REALTY ALLIANCE INC. DBA CENTURY 21 ALLIANCE FINEHOMES AND ESTATES' DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION ONE THROUGH FIVE. CCP 430.10(E) AND (F). DEFENDANT JENNY YING HE'S ACTIONS WERE PLED IN SUFFICIENT DETAIL TO ALLOW DEFENDANT REALTY ALLIANCE INC. DBA CENTURY 21 ("CENTURY 21") TO ANSWER TO THE COMPLAINT. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT CENTURY 21 WAS IN AN EMPLOYMENT OR AGENCY RELATIONSHIP WITH DEFENDANT HE. (FAC 6.) THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOT UNCERTAIN AS TO DEFENDANT CENTURY 21, AND DEFENDANT CENTURY 21 HAS SHOWN NO REASON WHY IT CANNOT REASONABLY RESPOND TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. CENTURY 21 ARGUES THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, BREACH OF CONTRACT. PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THIS ARGUMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, COURT REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR #11918

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JENNY YING HE AND REALTY ALLIANCE INC. DBA CENTURY 21 ALLIANCE FINE HOMES' MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE MOTION TO STRIKE ALL REQUESTS FOR EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS GRANTED (PARAGRAPHS 22, 29, 34, 40, 46, 53, AND PAGE 9, PRAYER 1). EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS NOT RECOVERABLE IN CASES ARISING SOLELY OUT OF PROPERTY DAMAGE, AND PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN NO EXCEPTION ON POINT. SEE ELRICH V. MENEZES, 21 CAL. 4TH 543, 554 (1999). GODFRY V. STEINPRESS, 128 CAL. APP. 3D. 154 (1982) CITED BY PLAINTIFF, IS DISTINGUISHABLE ON ITS FACTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, COURT REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR #11918

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JENNY HE, REALTY ALLIANCE INC. DBA CENTURY 21 ALLIANCE FINE DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS A DUPLICATE ENTRY. Judge: Marla Miller, Court Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR #11918

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Adam Pedersen, Esq., from Carlson Law Group, 818-996-7800, for Defendants and moving party

No other Appearance. Tentative Ruling is adopted. DEFENDANTS JENNY YING HE AND REALTY ALLIANCE INC. DBA CENTURY 21 ALLIANCE FINEHOMES AND ESTATES' DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
The demurrer is overruled as to causes of action one through five. CCP 430.10(e) and (f). Defendant Jenny Ying He's actions were pled in sufficient detail to allow Defendant Realty Alliance Inc. dba Century 21 ("Century 21") to answer to the complaint. The First Amended Complaint alleges that Century 21 was in an employment or agency relationship with Defendant He. (FAC 6.) The First Amended Complaint is not uncertain as to Defendant Century 21, and Defendant Century 21 has shown no reason why it cannot reasonably respond to the First Amended Complaint. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for breach of contract. Century 21 argues that Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to support the sixth cause of action, breach of contract. Plaintiff concedes this argument. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller, Court Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR #11918

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Adam Pedersen, Esq., from Carlson Law Group, 818-996-7800, for Defendants and moving party

No other Appearance. Tentative Ruling is adopted. DEFENDANTS JENNY YING HE AND REALTY ALLIANCE INC. DBA CENTURY 21 ALLIANCE FINE HOMES' MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
The motion to strike all requests for emotional distress is granted (paragraphs 22, 29, 34, 40, 46, 53, and Page 9, prayer 1). Emotional distress is not recoverable in cases arising solely out of property damage, and Plaintiff has shown no exception on point. See Elrich v. Menezes, 21 Cal. 4th 543, 554 (1999). Godfry v. Steinpress, 128 Cal. App. 3d. 154 (1982) cited by Plaintiff, is distinguishable on its facts. Order signed in open Court.Judge: Marla Miller, Court Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR #11918
